claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for one child. My son Alex, he's 15 and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you're responsible for your 15-year-old son Alex who lives with you.

Can you confirm that you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Does Alex have any circumstances that might be relevant, such as:
- Is he currently in hospital or care?
- Does he work 24 hours or more a week?
- Does he receive any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to Alex. He's not in hospital or care, he doesn't work that many hours - just lives at home with me like a normal 15-year-old. And no, he doesn't get any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- He has no disqualifying circumstances (not in extended hospital/care, not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in his own right)

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, you would still be eligible to claim Child Benefit - you would just need to consider whether the tax charge affects you.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government's claiming process. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for him. He is not in hospital or care for extended periods, does not work 24 hours or more per week, and does not receive benefits in his own right. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 15), who lives with them in the UK. Alex meets all the standard eligibility criteria: he is under 16, lives with the claimant, has no disqualifying circumstances (not in extended hospital/care stays, not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in his own right). The claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
